findings.message: New data-store query path with no observability instrumentation. The get_item_by_name function performs a database lookup without metrics recording, span tracing, or latency observation. This makes it impossible to monitor query performance or diagnose latency issues in production.
findings.suggestion: Instrument this database access path with OpenTelemetry spans and/or metrics similar to how request-level observability is handled in the middleware. Consider adding a span wrapper around the session.scalars call to track query latency and success/failure.

findings.message: Unindexed database query on Item.name field. The WHERE clause filters on 'name' without a corresponding database index, causing a full table scan. Combined with missing observability, query slowdowns will be invisible until user-facing latency occurs.
findings.suggestion: Add a database index on the Item.name field (e.g., 'name' as a unique or indexed column in the Item model) to ensure query performance scales with table size.
